name: RecordMetadata
statement: Parameterized constructor: stores topicPartition, timestamp, and serialized key/value sizes; computes offset as baseOffset+batchIndex, but keeps baseOffset as-is when it is -1 (unknown).

name: RecordMetadata
statement: No-arg constructor: not declared in this final immutable class; the only constructor requires topicPartition, baseOffset, batchIndex, timestamp, and serialized key/value sizes.

name: hasOffset
statement: Returns true when a real offset was assigned by the broker, i.e. the stored offset differs from ProduceResponse.INVALID_OFFSET; false signals the offset is unknown.

name: offset
statement: Returns the record's offset within its topic-partition as acknowledged by the broker, or -1 (INVALID_OFFSET) when hasOffset() is false.

name: hasTimestamp
statement: Returns true when a valid timestamp is present, i.e. the stored timestamp differs from RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP; false otherwise.

name: timestamp
statement: Returns the record's timestamp (broker LogAppendTime or producer/record CreateTime depending on topic config), or -1 when hasTimestamp() is false.

name: serializedKeySize
statement: Returns the size in bytes of the serialized, uncompressed key, or -1 when the record's key was null.

name: serializedValueSize
statement: Returns the size in bytes of the serialized, uncompressed value, or -1 when the record's value was null.

name: topic
statement: Returns the name of the topic the record was appended to, delegating to the stored TopicPartition.topic().

name: partition
statement: Returns the partition number the record was sent to, delegating to the stored TopicPartition.partition().

name: toString
statement: Returns a human-readable representation formatted as 'topic-partition@offset', concatenating TopicPartition.toString() with the offset.

name: ProducerInterceptor
statement: Plugin interface (extends Configurable, AutoCloseable) letting users intercept/mutate records before publish; callbacks may run on multiple threads so impls must be thread-safe.

name: onSend
statement: Called on send() before key/value serialization and partition assignment; may mutate and return the record (must be deterministic for log compaction); chained in interceptor.classes order, exceptions caught/logged not propagated.

name: onAcknowledgement
statement: Default no-op called when a record is broker-acknowledged or fails before send (just before user callback); runs on background I/O thread so must be fast; thrown exceptions are ignored.

name: close
statement: Abstract lifecycle hook invoked when the interceptor is closed, allowing the implementation to release any resources it acquired.

name: Callback
statement: A @FunctionalInterface a producer client implements to receive asynchronous notification when a sent record's request completes; runs on the background I/O thread so it must be fast.

name: onCompletion
statement: Invoked when the sent record is acknowledged: delivers RecordMetadata (partition/offset) on success, or a non-null Exception plus metadata with -1 in all fields on error (retriable or fatal).

name: Producer
statement: Generic message-producer interface Producer<K,V> extending Closeable; defines the contract a KafkaProducer/MockProducer implements to send keyed records and manage transactions.

name: initTransactions
statement: Initializes the producer's transactional state before any send; the no-arg default delegates to initTransactions(false), not retaining any previously prepared transaction.

name: beginTransaction
statement: Starts a new transaction so subsequent sends are bundled atomically; throws ProducerFencedException if a newer producer with the same transactional.id has fenced this one.

name: sendOffsetsToTransaction
statement: Atomically commits the given per-TopicPartition consumer offsets within the current transaction tied to the supplied ConsumerGroupMetadata; throws ProducerFencedException if fenced.

name: prepareTransaction
statement: Prepares (pre-commits) the current transaction and returns a PreparedTxnState capturing its state for later two-phase completion; throws ProducerFencedException if fenced.

name: commitTransaction
statement: Commits the in-flight transaction, atomically making all buffered records and sent offsets durable and visible; throws ProducerFencedException if this producer has been fenced.

name: abortTransaction
statement: Aborts the in-flight transaction, discarding all records and offsets sent since beginTransaction so none become visible; throws ProducerFencedException if fenced.

name: completeTransaction
statement: Completes a two-phase transaction from a prior PreparedTxnState, finalizing (committing or aborting) the prepared transaction; throws ProducerFencedException if fenced.

name: registerMetricForSubscription
statement: Registers the given KafkaMetric so it is included in client telemetry/metric subscriptions pushed to the broker.

name: unregisterMetricFromSubscription
statement: Removes the given KafkaMetric from the client's telemetry/metric subscription so it is no longer reported.

name: send
statement: Asynchronously buffers a ProducerRecord<K,V> for its topic/partition and returns a Future<RecordMetadata> completed on broker ack; an overload invokes a Callback on completion or error.

name: flush
statement: Blocks until all previously buffered records are sent and acknowledged, draining the accumulator so no record remains pending.

name: partitionsFor
statement: Returns the list of PartitionInfo metadata for the named topic, fetching cluster metadata if needed for partition-aware routing.

name: metrics
statement: Returns the producer's full map of MetricName to Metric instances for monitoring client performance and throughput.

name: clientInstanceId
statement: Returns the client's unique telemetry instance Uuid, blocking up to the supplied Duration timeout to obtain it from the broker.

name: close
statement: Closes the producer, flushing pending records and releasing resources; the no-arg form blocks indefinitely while an overload bounds the wait by a Duration timeout.

name: ProducerRecord
statement: Canonical 6-arg constructor: rejects null topic and negative timestamp/partition with IllegalArgumentException, stores fields, and wraps the headers Iterable in a RecordHeaders.

name: ProducerRecord
statement: Convenience constructors delegate to the 6-arg canonical one, defaulting unsupplied partition/timestamp/key/headers to null (e.g. topic+value yields a keyless, unpartitioned record).

name: topic
statement: Returns the mandatory, non-null destination topic name the record will be appended to.

name: headers
statement: Returns the record's Headers collection (a RecordHeaders built from the supplied Iterable, empty if none were provided), never null.

name: key
statement: Returns the optional record key used for partition selection via hashing, or null if no key was specified.

name: value
statement: Returns the record's payload contents, which may be null.

name: timestamp
statement: Returns the record's creation timestamp in milliseconds since epoch, or null when the producer should stamp it with System.currentTimeMillis() at send time.

name: partition
statement: Returns the explicit 0-indexed target partition, or null when no partition was specified so the producer chooses one by key hash or round-robin.

name: toString
statement: Renders a debug string 'ProducerRecord(topic=..,partition=..,headers=..,key=..,value=..,timestamp=..)', printing the literal 'null' for any absent field.

name: equals
statement: Two records are equal iff same instance or both ProducerRecords with null-safe equality across all six fields: key, partition, topic, headers, value, and timestamp.

name: hashCode
statement: Computes a hash by combining all six fields (topic, partition, headers, key, value, timestamp) with a 31-multiplier accumulation, treating null fields as 0, consistent with equals().

name: Partitioner
statement: Defines the producer's pluggable partitioning contract; an interface extending Configurable and Closeable so implementations are configured via partitioner.class and may register metrics under that config tag.

name: partition
statement: Computes and returns the target partition index for a record from its topic, key/keyBytes, value/valueBytes and current cluster metadata; key and keyBytes may be null when the record has no key.

name: close
statement: Lifecycle hook invoked when the partitioner is closed, letting the implementation release any resources it acquired during partitioning.

name: KafkaProducer
statement: Thread-safe Kafka client publishing records to a cluster; holds clientId, metrics, serializer/partitioner plugins, ProducerMetadata, RecordAccumulator buffer pool, background Sender ioThread, and optional TransactionManager.

name: KafkaProducer
statement: Constructs the producer from configs/Properties (+ optional key/value serializers): builds metrics, serializers, interceptors, metadata, accumulator, and Sender, starts the I/O thread; on any failure closes partial state and rethrows as KafkaException.

name: newSender
statement: Builds the background Sender: resolves max in-flight requests, request timeout, acks, and retries from config, creates or reuses the network client, and wires throttle metrics and the TransactionManager.

name: configureCompression
statement: Resolves the compression.type config into a Compression instance, applying the configured GZIP/LZ4/ZSTD level for those codecs, else default compression for the named type.

name: lingerMs
statement: Reads linger.ms config and clamps it to Integer.MAX_VALUE, returning the batch-linger wait in milliseconds as an int.

name: configureDeliveryTimeout
statement: Validates delivery.timeout.ms >= linger.ms + request.timeout.ms; throws ConfigException if the user explicitly set a smaller value, else silently raises it to that sum with a warning, returning the effective delivery timeout.

name: configureTransactionState
statement: If idempotence is enabled, builds a TransactionManager from transactional.id, 2PC flag, transaction.timeout.ms and retry backoff (logging transactional vs idempotent); otherwise returns null and ignores transaction configs.

name: initTransactions
statement: Initializes transactional producer state (fencing prior instances of the same transactional.id, fetching producer id/epoch); keepPreparedTxn=true enters 2PC recovery preserving prepared txns; blocks up to max.block.ms, then enables transaction V2.

name: beginTransaction
statement: Starts a new transaction on the TransactionManager after asserting a manager exists, the producer is open, and not in prepared state; records the begin-txn metric. Must follow initTransactions and precede each transaction.

name: sendOffsetsToTransaction
statement: Atomically enlists consumer-group offsets in the current transaction via the group coordinator (consume-transform-produce); blocks within remaining max.block.ms after awaiting topic metadata; offsets commit only when the transaction commits. No-op if offsets empty.

name: awaitTopicMetadata
statement: Requests a partial metadata refresh for the given topics, wakes the sender, and best-effort awaits the next update up to max.block.ms, returning elapsed wait millis so the caller can deduct it from its block budget; returns 0 if no update needed.

name: prepareTransaction
statement: Phase-one of 2PC: flushes pending messages and transitions the producer to prepared state where only commit/abort/complete are allowed, returning a PreparedTxnState (producerId, epoch); throws InvalidTxnStateException if 2PC is not enabled.

name: commitTransaction
statement: Flushes unsent records then commits the ongoing transaction via the coordinator, blocking up to max.block.ms; rethrows the last irrecoverable send error so a failed send aborts the commit; on success all record callbacks have completed.

name: abortTransaction
statement: Aborts the ongoing transaction, discarding any unflushed messages; blocks up to max.block.ms and immediately throws if a prior send hit a fenced/authorization error. Used to reset state after a recoverable transactional error.

name: completeTransaction
statement: Finalizes a 2PC prepared transaction by comparing the supplied PreparedTxnState against the producer's current prepared state: commits if they match, aborts otherwise; throws InvalidTxnStateException if no transaction is prepared.

name: send
statement: Asynchronously sends a record (optionally with a Callback): runs producer interceptors' onSend on the record then delegates to doSend, returning a Future<RecordMetadata>; the single-arg form is equivalent to send(record, null).

name: throwIfProducerClosed
statement: Guards every operation by throwing IllegalStateException if the Sender is null or no longer running, i.e. the producer has been closed.

name: throwIfInPreparedState
statement: Throws IllegalStateException if a transactional producer is in the prepared (2PC) state, where only commit/abort/complete are permitted, blocking all other operations.

name: doSend
statement: Core async send: waits on topic metadata, serializes key/value, computes the partition, validates record size, appends to the accumulator (enlisting the partition in any active transaction), and wakes the sender when a batch fills; returns the append Future or a FutureFailure for ApiExceptions.

name: setReadOnly
statement: Marks the record's RecordHeaders read-only (if of type RecordHeaders) before append so headers cannot be mutated once the record enters the send pipeline.

name: waitOnMetadata
statement: Blocks until cluster metadata for the topic (and optional specific partition) is available, issuing repeated metadata refresh requests up to maxWaitMs; rejects invalid topics, throws TimeoutException on expiry, and returns the cluster plus elapsed wait time.

name: getErrorMessage
statement: Formats the metadata-timeout error string: reports the topic as not present when partition count is unknown, otherwise reports the specific partition as missing, including the elapsed maxWaitMs.

name: ensureValidRecordSize
statement: Throws RecordTooLargeException if the serialized record size exceeds max.request.size, or exceeds the total buffer.memory, preventing oversized records from being buffered.

name: flush
statement: Makes all buffered records immediately sendable (ignoring linger.ms) and blocks until previously sent records complete with callbacks invoked; throws KafkaException if called from the I/O thread (inside a callback) to avoid deadlock.

name: partitionsFor
statement: Returns the partition metadata (PartitionInfo list) for a topic, refreshing metadata until the topic is found or max.block.ms expires; rejects a null topic and throws TimeoutException if not found in time.

name: metrics
statement: Returns an unmodifiable live view of the producer's internal metrics map keyed by MetricName, reflecting subsequent changes to the underlying metrics.

name: registerMetricForSubscription
statement: Registers an application KafkaMetric for telemetry subscription (pushed to the broker) only if not already present; existing producer metrics are not overwritten and the call logs a skip.

name: unregisterMetricFromSubscription
statement: Removes a previously registered application metric from telemetry subscription via the telemetry reporter; a no-op (logged skip) when the metric was never registered.

name: clientInstanceId
statement: Returns the producer's stable unique client instance Uuid used for telemetry correlation, blocking up to the given timeout for the broker request; throws IllegalStateException if enable.metrics.push is false and IllegalArgumentException on negative timeout.

name: close
statement: Closes the producer, blocking until pending requests complete (or up to the given timeout, then force-closing and failing unsent records / aborting the in-flight transaction); when called from a callback degrades to a non-blocking close(0) to avoid self-join deadlock.

name: partition
statement: Computes the target partition for a record: uses the record's explicit partition if set, else a configured custom partitioner (rejecting negative results), else hashes the serialized key, else returns UNKNOWN_PARTITION to defer to built-in partitioning.

name: throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata
statement: Validates ConsumerGroupMetadata for sendOffsetsToTransaction: throws IllegalArgumentException if null, or if generationId > 0 while the member.id is the unknown sentinel.

name: throwIfNoTransactionManager
statement: Throws IllegalStateException when a transactional API is invoked without a configured transactional.id (no TransactionManager), guarding all transaction methods.

name: getClientId
statement: Test-visible accessor returning the producer's configured clientId string.

name: ClusterAndWaitTime
statement: Immutable value holder pairing the resolved Cluster metadata with the milliseconds spent waiting on metadata, returned by waitOnMetadata so callers can deduct the wait from their block budget.

name: ClusterAndWaitTime
statement: Constructs the holder by storing the given Cluster and the waitedOnMetadataMs elapsed-wait value in final fields.

name: FutureFailure
statement: A pre-completed failed Future<RecordMetadata> wrapping an ApiException as an ExecutionException, returned by doSend when a recoverable API error occurs so callers observe the failure via the future.

name: FutureFailure
statement: Constructs the failed future by wrapping the given exception in an ExecutionException stored for later retrieval.

name: cancel
statement: FutureFailure.cancel always returns false: an already-failed/completed send future cannot be cancelled.

name: get
statement: FutureFailure.get (both timed and untimed overloads) immediately throws the stored ExecutionException, surfacing the send error to the caller.

name: isCancelled
statement: FutureFailure.isCancelled always returns false since the failed future was never cancelled.

name: isDone
statement: FutureFailure.isDone always returns true because the failed future is already complete.

name: AppendCallbacks
statement: Per-record callback bridge implementing RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks: captures topic/headers/requested-partition, receives the accumulator-assigned partition, and fans completion out to interceptors and the user callback.

name: AppendCallbacks
statement: Constructs the callback bundle from the user Callback, interceptors, and record, extracting topic, headers, requested partition, and trace string up front so no record reference is held for the batch lifetime; null record yields read-only empty headers.

name: onCompletion
statement: On record completion, substitutes placeholder RecordMetadata if none provided, notifies interceptors via onAcknowledgement, then invokes the user callback (if any) with the metadata and exception.

name: setPartition
statement: Callback invoked by the accumulator to record the actually-assigned partition (asserting it is not UNKNOWN_PARTITION) and trace-log the append once the partition is known.

name: getPartition
statement: Returns the partition assigned during accumulator append (UNKNOWN_PARTITION until setPartition is called).

name: topicPartition
statement: Lazily builds and caches the TopicPartition from the topic and the resolved partition, preferring the assigned partition, then the record's requested partition, else UNKNOWN_PARTITION; null when topic is null.

name: ProducerConfig
statement: Producer configuration class defining the full ConfigDef of producer settings (acks, retries, idempotence, batching, serializers, transactions) with defaults, validators, and importance.

name: postProcessParsedConfig
statement: Override that post-processes parsed config: validates SASL, warns on disabled exponential backoff, refines reconnect-backoff, validates idempotence configs, then overrides client.id.

name: maybeOverrideClientId
statement: If user did not set client.id, derives one as 'producer-' + transactional.id, or 'producer-'+atomic sequence number when no transactional.id; otherwise keeps the user value.

name: postProcessAndValidateIdempotenceConfigs
statement: Enforces idempotence prerequisites: requires retries>0, acks=all (-1), and max.in.flight<=5; auto-disables idempotence on conflicts if not user-set, else throws; bars transactional.id without idempotence and timeout with 2PC.

name: parseAcks
statement: Normalizes the acks string: maps 'all' (case-insensitive) to '-1' and parses any other numeric value to its short string, throwing ConfigException on non-numeric input.

name: appendSerializerToConfig
statement: Returns a config copy with key/value serializer classes injected from passed instances; if an instance is null and no serializer is already configured, throws ConfigException requiring it be non-null.

name: ProducerConfig
statement: Public constructor building a ProducerConfig from a Properties or Map of producer settings by delegating to AbstractConfig with the static CONFIG ConfigDef.

name: configNames
statement: Static accessor returning the set of all defined producer configuration key names from the CONFIG ConfigDef.

name: configDef
statement: Static accessor returning a defensive copy (new ConfigDef) of the producer configuration definition so callers cannot mutate the shared CONFIG.

name: main
statement: CLI entry point that prints HTML documentation of all producer configs to stdout, anchoring each with a 'producerconfigs_' id prefix at indent level 4.

name: Message
statement: Public, stable generic interface Message<T> defining the read contract for a Pulsar message: its payload, metadata, keys, schema, replication and broker-entry fields.

name: getProperties
statement: Returns an unmodifiable view of the application-defined key/value String properties attached to the message.

name: hasProperty
statement: Returns true if the message carries a property with the given name, false if that property is not defined.

name: getProperty
statement: Returns the String value of the named application property, or null if that property was not defined on the message.

name: getData
statement: Returns the raw uncompressed message payload as a byte[], available even when using the type-safe Schema API.

name: size
statement: Returns the uncompressed message payload size in bytes as an int.

name: getValue
statement: Returns the deserialized typed value T of the payload, decoded according to the configured Schema.

name: getMessageId
statement: Returns the unique MessageId identifying this message; only set for consumer-received messages, otherwise null for client-produced messages.

name: getPublishTime
statement: Returns the publish timestamp (millis) recording when a client published the message.

name: getEventTime
statement: Returns the application-set event time (millis) for the message, or 0 if no event time was set via the builder.

name: getSequenceId
statement: Returns the sequence id associated with the message, typically set by the application via the message builder.

name: getProducerName
statement: Returns the name of the producer that produced this message, or null if the producer name is not set.

name: hasKey
statement: Returns true if a partitioning/routing key was set when the message was created, false otherwise.

name: getKey
statement: Returns the String key of the message used for routing/partitioning.

name: hasBase64EncodedKey
statement: Returns true if the message key is base64-encoded, false if it is a plain string key.

name: getKeyBytes
statement: Returns the key as byte[]: base64-decoded if the key was base64-encoded, otherwise the UTF-8 bytes of the plain string key.

name: hasOrderingKey
statement: Returns true if an ordering key was set when the message was created, false otherwise.

name: getOrderingKey
statement: Returns the message's ordering key as a byte[], used to preserve per-key delivery ordering.

name: getTopicName
statement: Returns the name of the topic the message was published to.

name: getEncryptionCtx
statement: Returns an Optional EncryptionContext holding encryption and compression info so applications can decrypt an encrypted payload.

name: getRedeliveryCount
statement: Returns the broker-maintained redelivery count (incremented on ack timeout, recalculated on topic ownership change) for this message.

name: getSchemaVersion
statement: Returns the schema version of the message as byte[] if it was produced with a schema, otherwise null.

name: getSchemaId
statement: Returns an Optional byte[] external schema ID (PIP-420) set in metadata when produced with an external schema, otherwise empty.

name: getReaderSchema
statement: Default method returning the Schema used to decode this message's payload; defaults to Optional.empty() unless a reader/consumer schema is associated.

name: isReplicated
statement: Returns true if the message was replicated from another (geo-replicated) cluster, false otherwise.

name: getReplicatedFrom
statement: Returns the name of the cluster from which this message was replicated.

name: release
statement: Releases the message back to the buffer pool; only effective for consumers created with message pooling, otherwise a no-op.

name: hasBrokerPublishTime
statement: Returns true if the message carries a broker publish time from broker entry metadata, false otherwise.

name: getBrokerPublishTime
statement: Returns an Optional broker publish time from broker entry metadata, or empty if that broker feature is not enabled.

name: hasIndex
statement: Returns true if the message has an index from broker entry metadata, false otherwise.

name: getIndex
statement: Returns an Optional index from broker entry metadata, or empty if that broker feature is not enabled.

name: MessageRouter
statement: Public stable Serializable interface a producer accepts via ProducerBuilder.messageRouter() to plug custom partition-selection logic deciding which partition each published message targets.

name: choosePartition
statement: Deprecated (since 1.22.0) default overload taking only Message; throws UnsupportedOperationException, forcing callers to the metadata-aware choosePartition(Message, TopicMetadata) instead.

name: ProducerBuilder
statement: Generic builder interface (parameterized by message payload type T) that configures and creates Producer instances; extends Cloneable so partially-built configs can be branched.

name: create
statement: Synchronously finalizes and creates the Producer, blocking until ready; throws ProducerBusyException if a producer of the same name is already connected, or PulsarClientException on creation failure.

name: createAsync
statement: Asynchronously finalizes producer creation, returning a CompletableFuture yielding the Producer when ready; the future completes exceptionally with ProducerBusyException or PulsarClientException on failure.

name: loadConf
statement: Bulk-loads producer configuration from a String-keyed map (e.g. producerName, sendTimeoutMs), applying each entry to the builder, and returns the builder for chaining.

name: clone
statement: Returns a deep copy of the current builder so a shared incomplete configuration can be specialized independently (e.g. cloned then pointed at different topics) without mutating the original.

name: topic
statement: Sets the topic the producer will publish to; this is a required setting and must be provided before create()/createAsync().

name: producerName
statement: Assigns a custom producer name; if unset the system generates a globally unique one. Brokers enforce only one producer of a given name per topic, so the caller must keep it unique across clusters.

name: accessMode
statement: Sets the topic access mode: Shared (default, multiple producers), Exclusive (fail if one exists), ExclusiveWithFencing (evict existing producer), or WaitForExclusive (pend until exclusive access acquired).

name: sendTimeout
statement: Sets the send acknowledgment timeout (default 30s) in the given TimeUnit; if a message is not acked before it expires an error is reported. A value of 0 means infinite retry (no error propagated), enabling dedup.

name: maxPendingMessages
statement: Caps the number of unacknowledged messages held in the pending queue (default 0 = disabled), bounding client memory; when full, sends fail unless blockIfQueueFull is true.

name: maxPendingMessagesAcrossPartitions
statement: Deprecated; sets an aggregate upper bound on pending messages across all partitions, lowering each partition's maxPendingMessages if the total would exceed it (default 0 = disabled).

name: blockIfQueueFull
statement: Controls behavior when the pending queue is full: false (default) fails sends immediately with ProducerQueueIsFullError; true blocks the send/sendAsync call until space frees, simplifying high-rate publishing.

name: messageRoutingMode
statement: Sets the partition routing mode for a partitioned producer (default round-robin across partitions), applied only to keyless messages; keyed messages are routed by the hash of their key.

name: hashingScheme
statement: Selects the hashing function applied to a message key to choose its partition: JavaStringHash (default, String.hashCode) or Murmur3_32Hash.

name: compressionType
statement: Sets the payload compression codec (default NONE); supported values are LZ4, ZLIB, ZSTD (requires consumers >=2.3), and SNAPPY (requires consumers >=2.4).

name: compressionMinMsgBodySize
statement: Sets the minimum uncompressed body size in bytes (default 4KB) above which the configured compressionType is applied; smaller messages are sent uncompressed.

name: messageRouter
statement: Installs a custom MessageRouter implementation to override built-in partition selection logic for routing messages to partitions.

name: enableBatching
statement: Enables/disables automatic message batching (default enabled since 2.0.0); when on, multiple sendAsync calls coalesce into one broker batch (default 1ms delay, 1000 messages) for higher throughput and batch-level compression.

name: enableChunking
statement: Enables splitting oversized messages exceeding the broker's max-payload size into ordered chunks reassembled by the consumer; recommended with batching disabled and reduced maxPendingMessages.

name: chunkMaxMessageSize
statement: Sets the max per-chunk size in bytes triggering chunking when enabled (default -1, meaning chunk based on the broker-configured max message size).

name: cryptoKeyReader
statement: Sets a CryptoKeyReader used to supply keys for encrypting message payloads (end-to-end encryption).

name: defaultCryptoKeyReader
statement: Configures the default CryptoKeyReader from either a single public key string or a map of public-key-name-to-URI entries used to encrypt message payloads (since 2.8.0).

name: messageCrypto
statement: Sets a custom MessageCrypto implementation providing the encrypt/decrypt logic for end-to-end message encryption.

name: addEncryptionKey
statement: Adds a named public encryption key; at producer creation the client invokes the CryptoKeyReader's getPublicKey/getPrivateKey callbacks per key to load values (pkcs8), and encrypts after compression/batching.

name: cryptoFailureAction
statement: Sets the ProducerCryptoFailureAction dictating how the producer behaves when message encryption fails.

name: batchingMaxPublishDelay
statement: Sets the max time window (default 1ms) for which enabled batching holds messages before flushing; the batch is sent when this delay, the max-message count, or max-byte size is first reached.

name: roundRobinRouterBatchingPartitionSwitchFrequency
statement: Sets how often the round-robin router switches partitions while batching keyless messages (default 10); the switch period is frequency * batchingMaxPublishDelay, during which all messages go to one partition.

name: batchingMaxMessages
statement: Sets the max message count per batch (default 1000); when >1, messages queue until this count or the batch interval is reached, then publish as one ordered batch message.

name: batchingMaxBytes
statement: Sets the max byte size per batch (default 128KB); when >0, messages queue until this size or another batching condition is met, then publish as one ordered batch message.

name: batcherBuilder
statement: Sets the BatcherBuilder the producer uses to construct its batch message container; only effective when batching is enabled.

name: initialSequenceId
statement: Sets the sequence-id baseline; the first published message uses (initialSequenceId + 1) and subsequent messages get incremental ids unless explicitly specified.

name: property
statement: Attaches a single application-defined key/value metadata property to the producer, surfaced in topic/producer stats for identification.

name: properties
statement: Bulk-adds all entries of a property map as application-defined metadata on the producer, surfaced in topic/producer stats for identification.

name: intercept
statement: Registers one or more ProducerInterceptors to trace publish and acknowledgment operations; the varargs ProducerInterceptor<T> overload is deprecated in favor of the interceptor-package overload.

name: autoUpdatePartitions
statement: Enables/disables runtime auto-discovery of newly added partitions on partitioned topics (default true).

name: autoUpdatePartitionsInterval
statement: Sets how often partitions are re-checked for updates (default 1 minute) in the given TimeUnit; only effective when autoUpdatePartitions is enabled.

name: enableMultiSchema
statement: Enables/disables multi-schema mode (default enabled, since 2.5.0); when on the producer may send messages with a schema differing from the one at creation, otherwise an invalid-message exception is thrown.

name: enableLazyStartPartitionedProducers
statement: For Shared-mode producers on partitioned topics, toggles lazy partition startup: when enabled, partitions beyond the routing-chosen one connect on first message, reducing broker strain but adding first-message latency.

name: MessageId
statement: MessageId is an interface (no constructor); it defines sentinel instances earliest=newMessageId(-1,-1,-1) and latest=newMessageId(MAX,MAX,-1) for the oldest/next message positions, built via DefaultImplementation.

name: toByteArray
statement: Serializes this opaque message id into a byte[] so it can be persisted and later reconstructed via fromByteArray, without retaining the message content in memory.

name: fromByteArray
statement: Static factory that deserializes a MessageId from a serialized byte[] by delegating to DefaultImplementation.newMessageIdFromByteArray, throwing IOException if deserialization fails.

name: fromByteArrayWithTopic
statement: Static factory that deserializes a MessageId from a byte[] plus topic name (needed to acknowledge across multi-topic consumers) via DefaultImplementation, throwing IOException on failure.

name: ProducerAccessMode
statement: Enumerates the topic access mode a producer requests: Shared (multiple producers allowed), Exclusive (fail if another is connected), ExclusiveWithFencing (evict existing), WaitForExclusive (block until exclusive).

name: Producer
statement: Public stable generic interface Producer<T> extending Closeable; a single instance publishes typed messages to one topic and is safe to share across multiple threads.

name: getTopic
statement: Returns the name of the topic this producer is publishing to.

name: getProducerName
statement: Returns the producer's name, either auto-assigned by the system or the explicit name specified by the client at creation.

name: send
statement: Synchronously publishes a message, blocking until the broker acknowledges persistence, then returns its assigned MessageId; throws TimeoutException on timeout or AlreadyClosedException if closed.

name: sendAsync
statement: Publishes a message asynchronously, returning a CompletableFuture<MessageId> completed on the network I/O thread when persisted; fails with ProducerQueueIsFullError when the send queue is full and not configured to block.

name: flush
statement: Synchronously flushes all messages buffered in the client, blocking until every buffered message has been successfully persisted by the broker (since 2.1.0).

name: flushAsync
statement: Asynchronously flushes all client-buffered messages, returning a CompletableFuture<Void> that completes when all buffered messages are safely persisted (since 2.1.0).

name: newMessage
statement: Creates a typed message builder (TypedMessageBuilder<T>) for constructing a message with extra properties like key, value, and user-defined properties before sending.

name: getLastSequenceId
statement: Returns the last sequence id published and broker-acknowledged by this producer; after recreating with the same name returns the prior session's last id, or -1 if none was ever published.

name: getStats
statement: Returns interval and cumulative producer statistics (messages/bytes sent, send failures, acks received), or null when the stats recorder is disabled.

name: close
statement: Synchronously closes the producer and releases its resources, rejecting further writes and waiting for pending writes to persist without retrying on error; throws AlreadyClosedException if already closed.

name: closeAsync
statement: Asynchronously closes the producer and releases resources, returning a CompletableFuture<Void> that tracks completion; accepts no further writes and waits for pending writes to persist without retry.

name: isConnected
statement: Returns whether the producer currently has an active connection to the broker.

name: getLastDisconnectedTimestamp
statement: Returns the timestamp at which the producer was last disconnected from the broker.

name: getNumOfPartitions
statement: Returns the number of partitions for the producer's topic.

name: TypedMessageBuilder
statement: Generic Serializable producer-side builder interface (TypedMessageBuilder<T>) that accumulates message attributes via fluent setters, terminating in send()/sendAsync() to publish one message.

name: send
statement: Synchronously publishes the built message, blocking until the broker persists it, returning the broker-assigned MessageId; throws PulsarClientException on failure.

name: sendAsync
statement: Asynchronously publishes the message, returning a CompletableFuture<MessageId> completed on persistence; completes with ProducerQueueIsFullError when the producer queue is full.

name: key
statement: Sets the String partitioning key used by the producer's routing policy to choose the destination partition; returns the builder for chaining.

name: keyBytes
statement: Sets the routing/partitioning key as a raw byte array (base64-encoded internally) for the routing policy; returns the builder for chaining.

name: orderingKey
statement: Sets a byte-array ordering key governing dispatch ordering under Key_Shared subscriptions; the partition key is used instead when no ordering key is set. Returns the builder.

name: value
statement: Sets the typed domain-object payload (T) of the message; the value is required and is the only field loadConf cannot set. Returns the builder for chaining.

name: property
statement: Adds a single name/value String property to the message's metadata, available to consumers; returns the builder for chaining.

name: properties
statement: Bulk-adds all entries of the provided String-to-String map as message metadata properties; returns the builder for chaining.

name: eventTime
statement: Sets the application-defined event-time timestamp (long), retrievable via Message.getEventTime(); not indexed, so subscribers cannot seek by event time. Returns the builder.

name: sequenceId
statement: Assigns a custom non-negative, strictly-increasing sequence id for broker-side deduplication (need not be consecutive); returns the builder for chaining.

name: replicationClusters
statement: Overrides the geo-replication target clusters for this message to the supplied list, replacing topic defaults; returns the builder for chaining.

name: disableReplication
statement: Disables geo-replication for this message so it is not replicated to remote clusters; returns the builder for chaining.

name: deliverAt
statement: Schedules delayed delivery at/after the given absolute UTC millisecond timestamp; delay only applies to Shared/Key_Shared subscriptions, else delivered immediately. Returns the builder.

name: deliverAfter
statement: Schedules delayed delivery after the given relative delay+TimeUnit; honored only for Shared/Key_Shared subscriptions, else delivered immediately. Returns the builder.

name: loadConf
statement: Bulk-configures the builder from a map keyed by CONF_* constants (key, properties, eventTime, sequenceId, replication, delivery); cannot set the message value. Returns the builder.

name: MessageRoutingMode
statement: Public stable enum defining a partitioned producer's default key-less partition routing policy, with SinglePartition, RoundRobinPartition, and CustomPartition modes; applies only when a message has no key (keyed messages always hash the key to a partition).
